assertions.title: Support ticket submission

steps.expected: All unit tests pass covering ticket creation, GitHub async submission, and role-based scoping
steps.observed: Tests run: 4, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0 — all pass

steps.expected: Integration tests confirm POST /api/v1/support/tickets persists ticket and returns correct API envelope with category/subject/companyCode
steps.observed: Tests run: 3, Failures: 0, Errors: 0, Skipped: 0 — all pass. createTicket verifies local persistence and API response shape.

steps.expected: When GitHub is unavailable/disabled, ticket persists locally with error message and null githubIssueNumber
steps.observed: Test passes. Ticket saved with githubLastError containing 'disabled' and no githubIssueNumber.

steps.action: Code review: verify tenant context in GitHub issue body (SupportTicketGitHubSyncService.buildIssueBody)
steps.expected: Issue body includes company code and user ID for tenant context
steps.observed: Confirmed: body includes 'Company: {companyCode}', 'Requester User ID: {userId}', and 'Category: {category}'

steps.action: Code review: verify category->label mapping in GitHubIssueClient.createIssue payload
steps.expected: Per AGENTS.md: 'Labels from category (BUG->bug, FEATURE_REQUEST->enhancement, SUPPORT->support)' — payload should include 'labels' array
steps.observed: MISSING: GitHubIssueClient.createIssue() builds payload with only 'title' and 'body' keys. No 'labels' field is ever set. Grep for 'label' across all three service files (GitHubIssueClient.java, SupportTicketGitHubSyncService.java, SupportTicketService.java) returns zero matches. The category IS included in the issue title as '[CATEGORY] Subject' but is NOT mapped to GitHub labels.

codeReferences: GitHubIssueClient.java:createIssue() lines 43-46: payload only has 'title' and 'body', no 'labels'
SupportTicketGitHubSyncService.java:buildIssueTitle() — includes [CATEGORY] in title prefix but no label mapping
SupportTicketGitHubSyncService.java:buildIssueBody() — includes company code and user ID (tenant context OK)

assertions.issues: Category->label mapping to GitHub issue payload is MISSING. The validation contract and AGENTS.md require 'Labels from category (BUG->bug, FEATURE_REQUEST->enhancement, SUPPORT->support)' in the GitHub API payload. GitHubIssueClient.createIssue() only sends title and body — no 'labels' array is included. The category appears in the issue title as '[CATEGORY] Subject' but this is NOT equivalent to GitHub issue labels. All other aspects pass: local persistence, async GitHub push, graceful degradation, tenant context in body.

assertions.title: Support ticket status sync and notification

steps.expected: When GitHub issue is closed, local ticket status updates to RESOLVED, resolvedAt is set, email notification sent via ticket-resolved template
steps.observed: Test passes. Ticket status = RESOLVED, resolvedAt != null, resolvedNotificationSentAt != null, githubIssueState = 'CLOSED'. EmailService.sendTemplatedEmailRequired called with correct email, subject ('Support ticket resolved - #1201'), and template ('mail/ticket-resolved').

steps.expected: Scheduled job runs periodically to sync GitHub issue statuses
steps.observed: @Scheduled(cron = '0 */5 * * * *') — runs every 5 minutes. Queries up to 200 tickets with OPEN/IN_PROGRESS status that have githubIssueNumber. For each, fetches state from GitHub and updates local ticket.

steps.action: Verify graceful degradation during sync when GitHub unavailable
steps.expected: Sync errors are caught and recorded per-ticket without crashing the batch
steps.observed: Confirmed in code: try-catch blocks around each ticket sync handle both ApplicationException and RuntimeException, set githubLastError and continue to next ticket.

steps.expected: Users see own tickets; admins see all tenant tickets; superadmin sees all tickets globally
steps.observed: IT passes. RequesterA sees only own ticket (not teammate or foreign). AdminA sees own + teammate (not foreign). SuperAdmin sees all three (own, teammate, foreign).

steps.expected: Same role-based visibility rules apply to detail endpoint
steps.observed: IT passes. RequesterA gets 404 for teammate ticket, 200 for own. AdminA gets 200 for tenant tickets, 404 for foreign. SuperAdmin gets 200 for any ticket.

steps.expected: Thymeleaf template for resolved notification exists and uses layout wrapper
steps.observed: Confirmed: src/main/resources/templates/mail/ticket-resolved.html exists. Uses th:replace='~{mail/layout :: layout(~{::content})}' wrapper pattern. Includes ticket ID, subject, category, resolved time, and GitHub issue link.

steps.action: Verify re-open path (GitHub issue re-opened after resolution)
steps.expected: If closed issue is re-opened on GitHub, ticket moves back to IN_PROGRESS with resolvedAt/notification reset
steps.observed: Confirmed in code: updateTicketFromGitHubState() handles OPEN state on previously RESOLVED/CLOSED tickets — sets status=IN_PROGRESS, clears resolvedAt and resolvedNotificationSentAt.

codeReferences: SupportTicketGitHubSyncService.java:notifyResolved() — sends email via EmailService with ticket-resolved template
SupportTicketService.java:getById() — same role-based scoping for detail view
templates/mail/ticket-resolved.html — layout-wrapped template with ticket details

frictions.description: Compilation errors in unrelated test files (codered/CR_PeriodClose*, AccountingFacadeTest, AccountingPeriodService*Test, AccountingServiceStandardJournalTest) prevent running gate-fast profile or default test compilation. Required -Dmaven.compiler.testExcludes to isolate support ticket tests.
frictions.resolution: Used -Dmaven.compiler.testExcludes to exclude broken codered and accounting test files from compilation while running support ticket tests

summary: Tested 2 assertions: 1 passed (VAL-ADMIN-007), 1 failed (VAL-ADMIN-006). VAL-ADMIN-006 fails because the category->label mapping to GitHub issue payload is missing — GitHubIssueClient.createIssue() sends only title+body without a 'labels' array. All other aspects of ticket submission (local persistence, async GitHub push, graceful degradation, tenant context, role-based visibility) are working correctly. VAL-ADMIN-007 passes fully — scheduled sync, RESOLVED status on GitHub close, email notification, re-open handling, and role-based visibility all verified via unit and integration tests.
